gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One row of the `std.testing` guarantee matrix."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mirrors the `GuaranteeRow` shape from `mycelium-std-core` (spec ┬¦4 / README ┬¦2) ŌĆö kept"] # [doc = " local to avoid a circular crate dependency while `std.core` is not yet a dep."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct Row { # [doc = " The exported op name."] pub op : & 'static str , # [doc = " Guarantee tag on the `Exact ŌŖÉ Proven ŌŖÉ Empirical ŌŖÉ Declared` lattice (RFC-0001 ┬¦4.3)."] pub tag : GuaranteeStrength , # [doc = " Explicit fallibility: `\"total\"` or the `Option`/`Result`/`Verdict` shape returned."] pub fallibility : & 'static str , # [doc = " Declared effects (`\"none\"` for pure ops; `\"io (baseline read)\"` for golden)."] pub effects : & 'static str , # [doc = " Whether the op surfaces an EXPLAIN artifact (C3/SC-3/G11)."] pub explainable : bool , }
gaps.reason: struct `Row` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file ŌĆö emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `std.testing` guarantee matrix (spec ┬¦4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every row is `Exact` (the verdict mechanism is exact) and either effect-free or declares"] # [doc = " its IO effect explicitly (C6). EXPLAIN coverage is on every harness op that produces a"] # [doc = " non-`Pass` verdict (C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " | Op | Tag | Fallibility | Effects | EXPLAIN |"] # [doc = " |---|---|---|---|---|"] # [doc = " | `for_all` | Exact | `Fail{Diag}` / `Skipped{...}` as Verdict | none (pure; seeded) | yes |"] # [doc = " | `golden` | Exact | `Fail{diff}` / `Skipped{NeedsRecord}` | io (baseline read) | yes |"] # [doc = " | `differential` | Exact | `Fail{lhs,rhs}` / `Skipped{BackendUnavailable}` | none / io per backend | yes |"] # [doc = " | `summarize` | Exact | total | none | yes |"] # [doc = " | `is_green` | Exact | total | none | yes |"] pub const MATRIX : & [Row] = & [Row { op : "for_all" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Verdict::Fail{record} / Verdict::Skipped{NeedsRecord}" , effects : "none (pure; seeded ŌĆö C6/RT3)" , explainable : true , } , Row { op : "golden" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Verdict::Fail{diff} / Verdict::Skipped{NeedsRecord}" , effects : "io (baseline read ŌĆö declared, C6)" , explainable : true , } , Row { op : "differential" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "Verdict::Fail{lhs,rhs} / Verdict::Skipped{BackendUnavailable}" , effects : "none / io per backend (declared, C6)" , explainable : true , } , Row { op : "summarize" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "total" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } , Row { op : "is_green" , tag : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , fallibility : "total" , effects : "none" , explainable : true , } ,] ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const MATRIX` ŌĆö co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)
